admin.col.name: Name
join.contact.body: FreeTetra is in development. For questions, joining, or setup help: do1xx@pm.me
join.contact.title: Contact
join.help.body1: There's an active BlueStation Telegram group where hams offer boards, discuss issues and answer setup questions. Find the invite link via the MidnightBlueLabs repo or community.
join.help.body2: For FreeTetra-specific questions (server connect, TG schema): see contact below.
join.help.title: Help with BlueStation hardware/software
join.path1.acct_body: You need a RadioID (= DMR ID). If you don't have one yet: register there with your amateur radio callsign. Takes 1-2 days. Your TETRA ISSI is your RadioID + 2-digit SSID (e.g. 2623563 + 00 = 262356300).
join.path1.boards: Boards are available ready-made in the BlueStation Telegram group (where hams trade used and new ones) or you can have the open designs manufactured at JLCPCB.
join.path1.cfg_autoauth: On startup your radio automatically registers — no account signup needed, RadioID is verified against radioid.net.
join.path1.cfg_comment_pw: shared key for all RadioID users
join.path1.cfg_intro: This is the only FreeTetra-specific section in your config.toml:
join.path1.cfg_rest: The rest of the BlueStation config (SDR frequencies, [net_info] with MCC/MNC matching your radio, [cell_info] etc.) is BlueStation user setup, documented in the BlueStation docs.
join.path1.hw_ant: UHF antenna (a small stub antenna is enough for a hotspot)
join.path1.hw_misc: SD card, power supply, optionally a case
join.path1.hw_sx: SX1255 RF board as RPi HAT — several open designs exist (TetroPi, SXceiver by OH2EAT, more in the community)
join.path1.intro: The easiest way to start. No own server needed — just a BlueStation connecting to freetetra.de.
join.path1.sw_body: BlueStation by MidnightBlueLabs (Apache 2.0). A hotspot user needs nothing extra — no fork, no special build setup. Pre-built binaries or compile yourself as described in the README.
join.path1.title: Path 1: Hotspot with BlueStation
join.path2.fed_body: Federation runs on a symmetric shared key. For a new server to peer with the open FreeTetra network, it simply uses:
join.path2.fed_note: The key is deliberately public — FreeTetra is open, anyone can join. If you want to build a private mesh (e.g. only for your club, no connection to the public network), simply choose your own FEDERATION_KEY and only share it with your own peers.
join.path2.intro: If your group (club, association, regular round) wants their own local cluster: you run a FreeTetra server, your BlueStations connect there, you peer with freetetra.de and other FreeTetra servers.
join.path2.need_sw: FreeTetra server software (Go binary; repo goes public once stable)
join.path2.need_vm: Linux VM or small server (1 vCPU, 512 MB RAM is enough)
join.path2.title: Path 2: Your own server
join.path_hotspot: As a user with your own hotspot
join.path_hotspot.desc: A small BlueStation at home, for you + your handheld. The common case.
join.path_server.desc: Your own FreeTetra server for a local cluster (club, group, association).
join.tagline: FreeTetra is open — every licensed amateur radio operator can join. Here's how.
join.tgs.title: Talkgroups
join.title: Join
join.two_ways.title: Two paths
landing.about.title: About this server
landing.connect.full_doc: → Full guide: Join with your own BlueStation
landing.connect.intro: BlueStation config — just point the brew host to this server:
landing.connect.note: No registration required! Your RadioID is verified automatically. Password: blafablafa
landing.connect.title: Connect a server
landing.tagline: Free, federated TETRA network for amateur radio
landing.tgs.bm.desc: Like 10-90 + DMR bridge to BrandMeister. TG numbers map 1:1 (e.g. TG 262 = Germany, TG 2621 = DL Cluster North).
landing.tgs.bm.name: BrandMeister bridge
landing.tgs.global.desc: All FreeTetra servers worldwide, via Brew federation.
landing.tgs.global.name: FreeTetra global
landing.tgs.local.desc: Stays on this server — NOT federated to other FreeTetra servers. Distributed within the server to all connected cells. By convention: TG 7-9 for service bots (echo, weather, etc.) — each server operator runs their own.
landing.tgs.local.name: Server-local
landing.tgs.services: Currently active services: TG 9 Echo/parrot (server-local — each server operator should run their own echo on TG 9 to avoid federation ping-pong).
landing.tgs.title: Talkgroups (GSSI schema)
landing.what_is.based: Based on BlueStation (open source TETRA base station, Apache 2.0).
landing.what_is.body1: Free, federated TETRA network for amateur radio. Anyone can run their own server — servers connect to each other and share selected talkgroups. Login with your RadioID, no central account needed.
landing.what_is.body2: FreeTetra is both the project and the first server: what runs here on {{HOST}} you can host yourself the same way and peer with other FreeTetra servers.
landing.what_is.title: What is FreeTetra?
landing.whats_up.intro: Watch live who's transmitting and where there is coverage:
landing.whats_up.live.desc: Who spoke last — callsign, ISSI, talkgroup, duration. Live update every 2 sec.
landing.whats_up.live.name: Last Heard
landing.whats_up.map.desc: Where radios were successfully seen — built from LIP positions, aggregated into H3 hexagons (street/city/region depending on zoom).
landing.whats_up.map.name: Coverage Map
landing.whats_up.title: What's happening on the network
landing.whats_up.ui.desc: Full overview: TMO-sites, subscribers, federation peers, SDS console.
landing.whats_up.ui.name: Dashboard
live.title: FreeTetra Live
map.title: FreeTetra Map
